epics.title: Phase 1: Checkpoint-driven run with review gates

tasks.title: Add js-yaml dependency
tasks.description: WHAT: Add js-yaml as a direct dependency and @types/js-yaml as a dev dependency. Currently js-yaml is only a transitive dep of gray-matter and not importable under pnpm strict mode.
tasks.design: HOW: Run pnpm add js-yaml && pnpm add -D @types/js-yaml. Verify import works with a simple smoke test: import { dump, load } from 'js-yaml'.
acceptance:
- [ ] js-yaml appears in package.json dependencies
- [ ] @types/js-yaml appears in package.json devDependencies
- [ ] import { dump, load } from 'js-yaml' resolves without error

tasks.title: Checkpoint module with types and I/O
tasks.description: WHAT: Create the checkpoint module that defines the PipelineCheckpoint type and provides read/write/clear functions for plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml. This is the core data layer that the CLI and skill prompt depend on.
tasks.design:
HOW:

Types in src/lib/checkpoint.ts:
  type PipelineStepName = 'plan' | 'plan-review-gate' | 'tasks' | 'tasks-review-gate' | 'execute' | 'docs' | 'pr'
  type PipelineStepStatus = 'pending' | 'in-progress' | 'complete' | 'failed'
  
  interface ReviewResult {
    verdict: 'PASS' | 'FAIL'
    critical: number
    major: number
    minor: number
    issues: string[]  // one-liner per issue
  }
  
  interface PipelineStep {
    status: PipelineStepStatus
    passes?: number           // only for review-gate steps
    lastResult?: ReviewResult // only for review-gate steps
  }
  
  interface PipelineCheckpoint {
    feature: string
    started: string  // ISO timestamp
    steps: Record<PipelineStepName, PipelineStep>
  }

Constants:
  MAX_REVIEW_PASSES = 7
  PIPELINE_FILENAME = 'pipeline.yaml'
  PIPELINE_STEPS: PipelineStepName[] = ['plan', 'plan-review-gate', 'tasks', 'tasks-review-gate', 'execute', 'docs', 'pr']

Functions:
  readCheckpoint(feature: string, cwd?: string): PipelineCheckpoint | null
    - Resolves plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml via resolveRepoRoot
    - Returns null if file missing or YAML malformed
    - Uses js-yaml load() for parsing
  
  writeCheckpoint(feature: string, checkpoint: PipelineCheckpoint, cwd?: string): void
    - Writes to plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml using js-yaml dump()
    - Creates parent dir if needed
  
  clearCheckpoint(feature: string, cwd?: string): void
    - Deletes plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml
    - No-op if file doesn't exist
  
  createCheckpoint(feature: string): PipelineCheckpoint
    - Returns a new checkpoint with all steps set to pending and started = now
acceptance:
- [ ] readCheckpoint returns null when no pipeline.yaml exists
- [ ] readCheckpoint returns a valid PipelineCheckpoint when pipeline.yaml exists
- [ ] readCheckpoint returns null on malformed YAML (does not throw)
- [ ] writeCheckpoint creates/overwrites plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml with valid YAML
- [ ] Checkpoint YAML includes feature name, started timestamp, and all 7 steps with status
- [ ] Review-gate steps include passes count, last verdict, severity counts, and issue one-liners
- [ ] clearCheckpoint deletes plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml
- [ ] clearCheckpoint does not throw if file doesn't exist
- [ ] createCheckpoint returns checkpoint with all steps pending and current timestamp
- [ ] Round-trip: writeCheckpoint then readCheckpoint returns equivalent data

tasks.title: CLI: --reset flag and checkpoint display in forge run
tasks.description: WHAT: Extend forge run CLI to support --reset flag and display checkpoint state on resume. When --reset is present, delete the checkpoint and exit (bypassing all precondition checks). When resuming, print which steps are complete and where the pipeline will resume. Include checkpoint in --json output.
tasks.design:
HOW: Modify src/commands/run.ts:

The existing code already extracts feature via args.find(a => !a.startsWith('-')), which naturally skips flags like --reset. Use this same pattern — no need for separate pre-extraction parsing.

1. Check for --reset in args (args.includes('--reset')).
2. Extract feature using existing pattern: args.find(a => !a.startsWith('-')).
3. If --reset:
   - If no feature: print 'Usage: forge run --reset <feature-name>', exit(1)
   - Call clearCheckpoint(feature, cwd) — no precondition checks needed
   - Print 'Checkpoint cleared for <feature>'
   - Return early
4. Continue with existing precondition checks (PRD, forge.json, git clean).
5. After precondition checks, call readCheckpoint(feature, cwd).
6. If checkpoint exists:
   - Find first step with status != 'complete'
   - If that step has status 'failed': print failure info (step name + lastResult.issues), exit(1)
   - Print 'Resuming from step: <stepName> (steps 1-N already complete)'
7. For --json output: add checkpoint field (PipelineCheckpoint | null) to the response object.
   Shape: { status: 'ready', feature, checks, steps, checkpoint }

Import readCheckpoint, clearCheckpoint from '../lib/checkpoint'.
acceptance:
- [ ] forge run --reset <feature> deletes checkpoint and exits with confirmation — bypasses precondition checks
- [ ] forge run --reset with no feature prints usage error and exits with code 1
- [ ] forge run <feature> with no checkpoint shows full pipeline steps (existing behavior preserved)
- [ ] forge run <feature> with partial checkpoint prints 'Resuming from step: <step>' — does not prompt for confirmation
- [ ] forge run <feature> with failed step in checkpoint prints failure info and exits
- [ ] forge run <feature> --json includes checkpoint field (null when no checkpoint)

tasks.title: Rewrite run.md skill prompt and update run-process.md guidance
tasks.description: WHAT: Rewrite plugin/commands/run.md to include the full 7-step pipeline with review gates on plan and tasks creation, checkpoint maintenance throughout, and resume-from-checkpoint behavior. Also update guidance/run-process.md to document the review gates, 7-pass cap, checkpoint format, and restart behavior. These two files must be written together to stay consistent.
tasks.design:
HOW: Rewrite plugin/commands/run.md preserving the existing task agent, review loop, salvage agent, and error recovery sections. Add/change:

1. Pipeline overview section listing all 7 steps: plan, plan-review-gate, tasks, tasks-review-gate, execute, docs, pr

2. Checkpoint protocol section:
   - On start: run forge run <feature> to get state, then read plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml
   - If checkpoint exists with completed steps: skip them, resume from first incomplete
   - If checkpoint has in-progress review gate: resume at recorded pass number
   - If checkpoint has failed step: stop and report (the CLI already handles this)
   - If no checkpoint: create one with createCheckpoint, write it
   - After completing each step: update checkpoint status to complete, write file
   - After each review pass: update checkpoint passes count and lastResult, write file

3. Review gate protocol (for plan-review-gate and tasks-review-gate steps):
   - Update checkpoint status to in-progress
   - Self-review: run the self-review checklist inline (source coverage, placeholder scan, name consistency per review-gates.md)
   - Fix any issues found in self-review
   - Fresh-context review loop (max MAX_REVIEW_PASSES = 7 passes):
     - Spawn review agent via Agent tool with fresh context
     - Review agent uses plan-review or tasks-review criteria from review-gates.md
     - If PASS: update checkpoint lastResult with verdict PASS, mark step complete
     - If FAIL: update checkpoint with pass number, verdict FAIL, severity counts, issue summaries
     - Fix the issues
     - Loop
   - If 7 passes all FAIL: mark step failed in checkpoint, stop pipeline, report unresolved issues to user

4. Preserved sections (keep existing content):
   - Task Agent prompt template (TDD: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR)
   - Review Loop for task execution (the per-task review, distinct from artifact review gates)
   - Salvage Agent
   - Fix Agent
   - Error Recovery (DAG is source of truth, forge tasks ready for resume)

5. Updated Phase 0 Setup:
   - Step 1: Read/create checkpoint
   - Step 2: If plan step not complete, run /forge:plan process, mark complete
   - Step 3: Run plan-review-gate (review gate protocol above)
   - Step 4: If tasks step not complete, run /forge:tasks process, mark complete
   - Step 5: Run tasks-review-gate (review gate protocol above)
   - Step 6-8: execute, docs, pr (existing logic with checkpoint updates)
acceptance:
- [ ] run.md includes the 7-step pipeline with review gates
- [ ] run.md instructs the agent to read/create checkpoint at start
- [ ] run.md instructs the agent to skip completed steps on resume
- [ ] run.md instructs the agent to run self-review then fresh-context review loop for plan-review-gate and tasks-review-gate
- [ ] run.md specifies the 7-pass cap with escalation to failed status and human notification
- [ ] run.md instructs the agent to update the checkpoint after each step and review pass
- [ ] run.md instructs the agent to resume review loops at the correct pass number when checkpoint shows in-progress review gate
- [ ] run.md preserves existing task agent, review loop, salvage agent, and error recovery content
- [ ] run-process.md documents review gates for plan and tasks creation
- [ ] run-process.md documents the 7-pass cap as forge:run-specific
- [ ] run-process.md documents the checkpoint file format with example YAML
- [ ] run-process.md documents restart behavior (resume, failed, clearing)

tasks.title: Update run-process.md guidance with review gates and checkpoint docs
tasks.description: WHAT: Update guidance/run-process.md to document the review gates for plan and tasks creation, the 7-pass cap, and the checkpoint file format and restart behavior. This is the deep reference that the skill prompt points to.
tasks.design:
HOW: Add new sections to guidance/run-process.md:

1. Add 'Review Gates for Plan and Tasks' section after Prerequisites:
   - Explain that /forge:run now runs review gates on plan and tasks creation
   - Reference review-gates.md for the review protocol
   - Document the 7-pass cap as forge:run-specific (does not override review-gates.md general guidance which has no fixed cap)
   - Describe self-review + fresh-context review loop flow

2. Add 'Checkpoint File' section:
   - Location: plans/<feature>/pipeline.yaml
   - Format: YAML with feature, started timestamp, 7 named steps
   - Each step has status (pending/in-progress/complete/failed)
   - Review-gate steps have passes, lastResult (verdict, severity counts, issues)
   - Include the example YAML from the PRD

3. Add 'Restart Behavior' section:
   - On restart: read checkpoint, skip completed steps
   - In-progress steps: re-run (review gates resume at recorded pass number)
   - Failed steps: print failure info, exit
   - Missing/malformed checkpoint: treat as fresh start
   - Clearing: forge run --reset <feature>
acceptance:
- [ ] run-process.md documents review gates for plan and tasks creation
- [ ] run-process.md documents the 7-pass cap as forge:run-specific
- [ ] run-process.md documents the checkpoint file format with example YAML
- [ ] run-process.md documents restart behavior (resume, failed, clearing)
tasks.closeReason: Collapsed into FORGE-1.4 per review feedback — tightly coupled docs that should be written together
